74999.subject: The Anatomy of a Wallet Page
74999.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/the-anatomy-of-a-wallet-page

The last piece in this series covered SnowTrace, Avascan, and SnowScan and when to use each one.
This article focuses on what those tools actually show you. A wallet page is more than a collection of balances and transactions; it’s a record of behavior. Every balance, token, approval, and contract interaction is a clue.
The guiding principle is simple: Always watch for the weird, the unexpected, and the unprecedented.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
Address & Balance Overview
At the top of the page you’ll see the wallet address: a long 0x... string that serves as the account’s identifier on Avalanche’s C-Chain.
Sometimes explorers attach labels to known addresses such as exchanges, protocols, routers, or treasury wallets. If no label exists, all you know is that the address has no publicly recognized identity. Many explorers also allow users to create private name tags for addresses they frequently monitor. These labels are personal notes and do not verify the identity or legitimacy of an address.
Directly beneath the address, you’ll typically find the wallet’s AVAX balance and estimated USD value. By themselves, these figures reveal very little. A wallet may be active, dormant, newly created, or emptied after an attack. The important part is context.
You’ll also see convenience features such as copy buttons and QR codes. These make addresses easier to share but provide no security information.
Normal: A standard address, with or without a label, and balances that align with the wallet’s expected activity.
Warning: Explorer-generated scam warnings, known malicious addresses, or near-zero AVAX balances paired with large numbers of unfamiliar tokens.
Takeaway: The header provides context, not trust. Labels and balances can be useful clues, but the wallet’s activity is what matters most.
Token Holdings
Below the Total balance, you’ll see the wallet’s total balance and the number of assets it holds. Clicking the dropdown arrow expands the asset breakdown, revealing the tokens, NFTs, LP tokens, and other assets associated with the address.
What you find there falls into two categories: assets you acquired intentionally, and assets you didn’t.
Legitimate holdings are usually assets you intentionally acquired, such as stablecoins, LP tokens, governance tokens, or recognized currencies like USDC and WAVAX.
Unfamiliar entries are often spam tokens; worthless assets airdropped to wallets as part of scam campaigns.
The most common red flag here is the presence of unsolicited tokens.
Scammers frequently airdrop worthless tokens to random wallets. The goal is not the token itself but the interaction that follows. Users are encouraged to visit a website, connect a wallet, approve a transaction, or “claim” a reward. Those actions often lead to theft.
Many scam t

74998.subject: 💼 Claude is an employee now
74998.body:
----------
######                  [Collaborate with us](https://6hhfxazcbpc.typeform.com/to/uXIl0IFH?utm_source=atlas&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=general_2026_05_11&utm_content=banner_post)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** Claude now has a seat in your Slack, a coworker you tag for tasks that already writes most of its own team's code. Today also brings biology you can program like software, Meta hitting pause on the AI that spied on its own staff and Mistral out-reading the field on documents.



**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **Claude:** What happens when you can tag Claude in Slack like a coworker?

* **Biology:** What does it mean that you can now code DNA like software?

* **Meta:** Why did Meta pull the plug on its own employee-tracking AI?

* **Mistral:** Why is Europe suddenly winning document AI?


----------



----------
## [Claude joined your Slack](https://www.anthropic.com/news/introducing-claude-tag)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/399871ec-a3af-4f31-990d-5fbf2ced49a7/Beehiiv_01_-_Jun_24.png?t=1782294378)
Caption: 

Claude now has a seat in your Slack. Anthropic's new Claude Tag lets anyone in a channel tag @Claude, hand off a task and get back to work while it grinds through the thread. 

Give it the channels, tools, data and codebases you choose, and it holds the context and plans ahead.

Anthropic has run its own product team on the tool all year, and says it now writes 65% of their code, including most of itself. Read that twice. The coworker that builds the coworker.

Then there's access. 

Claude joins under one company identity with the keys to whatever an admin connects, so its reach is whatever an admin wires up, whoever does the tagging. 

You get per-channel limits, spend caps and an audit log of every request, and you will want all of it before you plug an agent into your tools and source code.

It's in beta on Team and Enterprise, and it kills the old Claude in Slack app on August 3. Useful, obviously. An agent this capable is also only as safe as the access you hand it.


----------







----------
## [The risky stock that turned $1,000 into $225,000](https://utm.genai.works/r/iluzgxs)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/57102138-6416-4abc-ad97-789048edc4e8/Beehiiv_02_-_Jun_24.png?t=1782294402)
Follow image link: (https://utm.genai.works/r/iluzgxs)
Caption: 

Ten years ago Nvidia was a chip company investors scrolled past. That $1,000 stake is worth about $225,000 now, pulled up by the AI boom.

The mone

74997.subject: Interactive Brokers just let Grok, Claude, and ChatGPT into your portfolio - on purpose 🤖📊; Meta wants to clone …
74997.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/fintechpulse1093

Hey Everyone,
Good morning & happy Wednesday! Today we’re diving into investing giant Interactive Brokers, which just let xAI’s Grok, Claude and ChatGPT into your portfolio (what IBKR’s new AI capabilities are all about, how it compares to what Robinhood & eToro have launched, and why it might be the most defensible play + bonus deep dive into Coinbase pivot to Financial OS for AI Agents [ https://substack.com/redirect/663d1f60-733d-4053-85c3-82d2f494d917?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], how Anthropic wants to be Wall Street’s OS [ https://substack.com/redirect/8e3dd6ac-8557-44f5-b467-e15da639395b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], and AI Playbook for Finance [ https://substack.com/redirect/97d9f540-6a74-438f-8a26-798fa6fbc856?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] inside), and Meta that now wants to clone Polymarket & Kalshi (how Zuck is using the same playbook all over again, and why this time it’s really different + bonus deep dive into Anthropic, which just told AI founders exactly what to build in 2026 [ https://substack.com/redirect/654b514b-23bc-45f5-9439-9872a1731b0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] inside). So let’s just jump straight into the interesting stuff 🌶️
Interactive Brokers just let Grok, Claude, and ChatGPT into your portfolio - on purpose 🤖📊
The news 🗞️ One of the most interesting brokerage (& FinTech to an extent) decisions this month wasn’t a fee cut, a new asset class, or a new stablecoin. It was a protocol choice.
Interactive Brokers $IBKR  just connected Grok (now part of SpaceX [ https://substack.com/redirect/39209746-120a-44df-a548-966b3335c656?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]), Claude, and ChatGPT to live trading accounts across 170+ global markets, all through MCP, a single open standard that makes the AI model layer interchangeable.
Interactive Brokers’ integration with Grok in action.
No other major broker has done this. FinTech competitors Robinhood [ https://substack.com/redirect/cbc68e22-13fd-4695-85b6-c343bdd439eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] and eToro [ https://substack.com/redirect/5859ec79-2a82-49b0-a6ef-d77a0c77cc00?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] are placing very different bets on how AI and trading converge.
Most importantly, not all three models survive contact with regulators. 
Let’s unpack this...

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9saW5hcy5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_dG9rZW49ZXlKMWMyVnlYMmxrSWpveE1Ea3dOVFV6TURnc0luQnZjM1JmYVdRaU9qSXdNekk1Tmprek55d2lhV0YwSWpveE56Z3lNamt6TkRVMUxDSmxlSEFpT2pFNE1UTTRNamswTlRVc0ltbHpjeUk2SW5CMVlpMDRNVGd4T1NJc0luTjFZaUk2SW1ScGMyRmliR1ZmWlcxaGFXd2lmUS5vbEZBY1ZUQzlWY1dUWGxEcVdkeWlhMmllaUozWWlpLXBQQ0hWVThoN

What’s changed since you left
-----------------------------

Crypto in Europe looks different than it did six months ago.

MiCA enforcement begins 1 Jul, 2026. A significant share of EU
crypto activity sits on platforms that will not be
MiCA-authorised after that date.

Kraken has held MiCA authorisation via the Central Bank of
Ireland and MiFID licensing for derivatives via the Cyprus
Securities and Exchange Commission (342/17) well in advance. We
have been operating since 2011, with quarterly Proof of Reserves
independently verified.

If you signed up with Kraken once, the exchange you signed up
with is now the licensed one.

And there is €1M up for grabs.

----------------------
How the €1M draw works
----------------------

Every euro you deposit before 31 Jul, 13:59 UTC earns one
entry into the €1M draw. No cap on entries. No catch. New and
existing users eligible across the EEA.

Forty days. One entry per euro.
